rulings.Case Title: MARY BETH PERNIZ VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Request For Leave To File An Answer-Inintervention
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 3, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Specially appearing American States Insurance Company's (A Safeco Company) motion to set aside the entry of default against Aladdin Heating Corporation and to seek leave to file an answer-in-intervention is granted. The entry of default against Aladdin Heating Corporation on June 30, 2014 is set aside solely as related to American States Insurance Company. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GIOVANNI MAUGERI ET AL VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Chevron Entities Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 3, 2019 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Parker Hannifan's joinder in Chevron Entities' motion to dismiss or stay for forum non conveniens is off calendar as moot because the underlying motion remains on calendar and will be ruled upon. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GIOVANNI MAUGERI ET AL VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By The Chevron Entities To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 3, 2019 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendants Chevron Entities' motion to dismiss or stay for forum non conveniens is denied. Defendants failed to demonstrate that Italy is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) Defendants also failed to show that the balance of private and public interest factors makes it "just" that the litigation proceed in the alternative forum. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
